title: Slovenia in Limbo as President Defers Decision on Prime Minister
summary: Slovenian President Natasa Pirc Musar said she won’t propose a candidate for prime minister after a first round of talks with political parties, as no-one seems to be able to gather the needed majority among lawmakers.

title: UniCredit Said to Grow Generali Stake to Shape Potential Deals
summary: UniCredit SpA’s decision to expand its stake in Assicurazioni Generali SpA is meant to support its commercial ties and strengthen the bank’s hand in any potential deals involving the insurer pursued by other players, according to people familiar with the matter.

title: Ricky Sandler Shuttering Eminence Capital After 27-Year Run
summary: Hedge fund veteran Ricky Sandler is shuttering Eminence Capital and returning investor cash after a 27-year run, telling clients it hasn’t performed well enough in recent years to keep operating.

title: Masters in Business: Motley Fool's David Gardner (Podcast)
summary: Source: Bloomberg, 0:00

title: China Warns of Heavy Rain, Flood Risks Ahead of May Day Holiday
summary: Chinese weather authorities warned of heavy rainfall across southern regions from April 26 to April 29, with downpours expected to hit areas already saturated by earlier storms, raising the risk of flooding and landslides.

title: Thousands Protest Against Outgoing Malaysian Anti-Graft Chief
summary: Thousands of Malaysians gathered in Kuala Lumpur on Saturday, reiterating calls for the establishment of a Royal Commission of Inquiry into allegations involving outgoing anti-graft chief Azam Baki.

title: The Stock Picking Philosophy to Find the Next Amazon: Masters in Business
summary: Barry speaks with David Gardner, Author and Co-Founder, at the Motley Fool. They discuss David's new book book "Rule Breaker Investing: How to Pick the Best Stocks of he Future and Build Lasting Wealth." They also discuss the pros and cons of different investing strategies and trends. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Russian Attack on Ukrainian Cities Kills Four People, Zelenskiy Says
summary: Russia carried out one of the largest attacks on Ukraine overnight, killing four people and leaving over 30 wounded across the country, Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskiy said on Telegram.

title: Malaysia Picks a Former Judge to Lead Its Anti-Graft Agency
summary: Malaysia named Abdul Halim Aman as the next anti-graft chief, taking over from embattled head Azam Baki when his term ends next month.

title: US Sanctions Giant Chinese Refiner Hengli Over Iran Links
summary: The US sanctioned one of China’s largest private oil refiners over its links to Iran, a move that ramps up economic pressure on Tehran but also risks irking Beijing just weeks ahead of an expected leaders’ summit.

title: US Grants Maduro Access to Funds for Defense in Criminal Case
summary: US officials reversed themselves and will now allow Nicolás Maduro and his wife to pay lawyers with Venezuelan funds as they defend themselves in the drug-trafficking case against them in New York.

title: Xi Calls Laos Key Priority in China’s Neighborhood Diplomacy
summary: Chinese President Xi Jinping said that Beijing has always viewed its relations with Laos from a strategic and long-term perspective, describing the country as a key priority in China’s diplomacy with neighboring nations.

title: China Says US Export Bills Risk Disrupting Chip Supply Chains
summary: China said it is closely monitoring US legislative plans after the House Foreign Affairs Committee advanced export-control bills targeting semiconductors, which Beijing warned could disrupt global supply chains if passed.

title: China Hit Brakes on Fiscal Stimulus as Economy Holds Up Amid War
summary: China pulled back on fiscal spending in March as the economy rebounded at the start of the year despite disruptions caused by the war in Iran.

title: AI Chip Surge Elevates Taiwan, Korea in Global Equity Rankings
summary: The artificial intelligence boom has triggered a seismic reshuffling of global equity markets, with Taiwan and South Korea muscling past European nations one by one.

title: Kentucky's Thomas Massie Stakes His 'MAGA' Claim
summary: Kentucky Representative Thomas Massie, a committed libertarian, is the rare Republican willing to publicly criticize Donald Trump—over domestic spending, the war in Iran, the president’s efforts to bury the Jeffrey Epstein files. And he’s done this while running for an eighth term in Kentucky’s deep-red 4th Congressional District, which gave Trump two-thirds of the vote in 2024. Bloomberg Businessweek National Correspondent Josh Green joins Bloomberg Businessweek Daily to discuss. He speaks with Carol Massar and Tim Stenovec.  (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Grindr CEO on Inaugural WHCD Party, Political Initiatives
summary: Grindr CEO George Arison joins Bloomberg Businessweek Daily to discuss the dating app's inaugural White House Correspondents' Dinner party, plus the company's presence in Washington, DC focused on HIV treatment and other health initiatives. Arison also discusses the company's newest tiers, including AI features. He speaks with Carol Massar and Tim Stenovec. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Intel Plans Multi-Billion Dollar Bond Sale Tied to Apollo Deal
summary: Intel Corp. is readying a multi-billion dollar bond sale to finance the repurchase of half of a plant in Ireland that it had previously sold to Apollo Global Management Inc.

title: US Life Insurers Have Shifted More General Account Risk Offshore
summary: US life insurers have shifted more of their general account risk to entities abroad than in the US as of year-end, the first time that offshore reinsurance hubs have overtaken domestic ones for that business.

title: Tax the Rich Policies Draw Attention
summary: Michael Bologna, Bloomberg Industry Group senior tax correspondent, joins Scarlet Fu on "Bloomberg Real Yield." Democrats around the country are pushing forward on plans to tax the rich — moves that are supportive for the tax-exempt municipal-bond market.   (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Fmr. Fed Governor Betsy Duke on Warsh's Future, Rates
summary: Former Federal Reserve Governor Betsy Duke discusses the Department of Justice dropping its investigation into Jerome Powell, what a possible Kevin Warsh led Fed could look like, and previews Wednesday’s rate decision. She talks with Romaine Bostick and Katie Greifeld on “The Close.” (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Private Credit Needs to Close the Trust Gap: Kasser
summary: Susan Kasser, head of private debt at Neuberger, joins Scarlet Fu on "Bloomberg Real Yield." The amount of new money private credit managers brought in during the first quarter plunged almost 60% from a year earlier, an added challenge for funds that are also grappling with unprecedented redemption requests. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: 'Pockets of Complacency' in Credit: Barings' Best
summary: Michael Best, high yield portfolio manager at Barings, joins Scarlet Fu on "Bloomberg Real Yield." Attractive yields and tight spreads produced a supply surge in the US high yield market this week. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Brazil Blocks Polymarket, Kalshi Over ‘Illegal Betting’
summary: Brazil’s government blocked access to Polymarket and Kalshi, part of a crackdown on prediction market platforms it said were out of compliance with federal gambling laws.

title: Belgium Cut by S&P in Second Judgment on Worst Euro-Zone Deficit
summary: Belgium was cut by S&P Global Ratings, the second downgrade by a credit assessor in a week on the country running the euro area’s biggest budget deficits.

title: Wall Street Conviction Frays on Trades Riding Out AI, War Shock
summary: One by one, high-conviction trades on Wall Street are falling out of favor.

title: Nasdaq 100, S&P 500 Finish at Record on Intel’s Blowout Results
summary: US stocks finished at all-time highs on Friday as a blockbuster sales forecast from Intel Corp. fueled a rally in technology stocks, while trader optimism around the prospect of US-Iran talks increased.

title: Record Day for S&P, Nasdaq | Closing Bell
summary: Comprehensive cross-platform coverage of the U.S. market close on Bloomberg Television, Bloomberg Radio, and YouTube with Romaine Bostick, Katie Greifeld, Carol Massar and Tim Stenovec. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: DOJ Drops Powell Probe, Iran War Impact on Credit Markets | Real Yield 4/24/2026
summary: "Bloomberg Real Yield" highlights the market-moving news you need to know. Today's guests: BMO Capital Markets Head of US Rates Strategy Ian Lyngen, Moody's Rating Global Head of Private Credit Marc Pinto, Former Federal Reserve General Counsel Scott Alvarez, Barings High Yield Portfolio Manager Michael Best, and Neuberger Head of Private Debt Susan Kasser.  (Source: Bloomberg)

title: ‘The Jig Is Up’: 70% Crash Marks Quick End to Avis Short Squeeze
summary: The speculative mania around Avis Budget Group Inc. erupted quickly. It collapsed even faster.
